LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. COUNSEL STATE THAT THEY HAVE REACHED A RESOLUTION: DEFENDANT HAS AGREED TO SUBMIT TO THE TENTATIVE AND IN EXCHANGE, PLAINTIFF WILL DISMISS DEFENDANT WITHOUT PREJUDICE, IN EXCHANGE FOR MUTUAL WAIVER OF COSTS FROM PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR FALSE REPRESENTATION, AND INTENTIONAL TORT, NAMELY FRAUDULENT CONCEALMENT. ALSO, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OBJECTIONS TO JOHNSON DECLARATION AND AY DECLARATION OVERRULED. OBJECTION TO CIABATTARI DECLARATION SUSTAINED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED EXCEPT SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED REGARDING PUNITIVE DAMAGES. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED THAT DECEDENT WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS FROM WORK BY PLANT AND ITS WORK WITH ASBESTOS CONTAINING INSULATION. THE COURT ADDS THAT COUNSEL STIPULATION ABOVE, IS PART OF THE COURT'S RULING. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO PREPARE ORDER FOR COURT'S SIGNATURE. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-02-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Kimberly J. Chu, of Brayton Purcell LLC, (415) 898-1555, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Hong T. Le, Esq., of McKenna Long & Aldridge, LLP, (415) 4124, appearing for Defendant Plant Insulation. Counsel state that they have reached a resolution: Defendant has agreed to submit to the tentative and in exchange, plaintiff will dismiss defendant without prejudice, in exchange for mutual waiver of costs from plaintiff's claim for false representation, and intentional tort, namely fraudulent concealment. Also, The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Objections to Johnson declaration and Ay declaration overruled. Objection to Ciabattari declaration sustained. Motion for Summary Judgment and summary adjudication denied except summary adjudication granted regarding punitive damages. Triable issue of fact raised that decedent was exposed to asbestos from work by Plant and its work with asbestos containing insulation. The Court adds that counsel stipulation above, is part of the Court's ruling. Plaintiff's counsel to prepare Order for Court's signature. (302CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
